## [12:11 - 15:16]

different from platform to platform, wouldn't you be able to get more context based on the platform placement you use? So the reason that I use automatic and all placements whenever I can, there are a few reasons. Number one is social proof. So again, we'll learn more about this over the coming weeks, but we always try to create copy and creative that's going to work on as many placements and Facebook platforms as possible so that we can aggregate as much social proof as we can onto a few ads. Another reason we do this is because we want to play into how Facebook's algorithm works, which is, you know, Facebook, as we talked about last Thursday, the where we are with the machine learning is actually really advanced. And the more that we try to hack and kind of overthink this and create, you know, a campaign for each placement and try to force reach, it just doesn't work. We want to allow Facebook to optimize this to the best of their abilities. We're giving Facebook an objective, we're giving them assets to work with, and they know what the goal of our campaign is. So instead of us saying, Hey, let's show over here on this placement or let's show over here on this placement, let's allow Facebook to optimize the campaign or the ad sets and show the ads on the placements that are working the best. ## [36:41 - 39:45]

know, right? Like let me give you an example. In the Christian market, right, guess what day is the worst? It's Sunday because people are with their family. They are at church. one. Do we think it's the copy, the creative, the offer, the targeting, the campaign structure? We have a system for showing you guys how to do this. It's pretty detailed. So, you know, I mean, if you are using CBO, something that you could do, I mean, we're all facing fatigue in some way. So you need to think Facebook is showing your ads to the most low-hanging fruit humans based off of your objective and your targeting. So, you know, we're always facing fatigue. Because then those ads can be shown to people who have already seen the previous ads, but haven't taken that action. is of this age group, but naturally her hair just has not turned gray yet. it's like, oh, well, this isn't working. Like we're not going to give this anymore reach or this is just severely changing the optimization of the campaign.
